synthetic:0fbbae0429f6dd5bd67ce72d371093.reason: The dependabot.yml configuration lacks a `cooldown` block under both package-ecosystem entries (gomod at lines 4-10, github-actions at lines 14-20). Without a cooldown period, Dependabot will propose updates to newly published package/action versions immediately, increasing exposure to supply-chain attacks where a malicious version is published and picked up before it can be flagged/pulled by the community. This matches the rule's exact detection criteria and the file confirms the absence of the recommended `cooldown: default-days: 7` block on the flagged github-actions entry (and the sibling gomod entry).

synthetic:4fe6c0467edb31027eebf2f8a41aa9.reason: math/rand/v2 is imported solely to add jitter to HTTP retry backoff delays (internal/agent/openai.go:278, used by backoff() at 272-279, called from the retry loop in post() at 180-200). This value only affects timing between retried HTTP requests to a chat-completions endpoint; it is never used to generate secrets, tokens, keys, or any security-relevant identifier, and no attacker-controlled data flows into or out of the rand call. Use of a non-cryptographic PRNG for retry jitter is not a security weakness.

synthetic:a46b9fb36f28a5d90c3d72434c68c6.reason: The dependabot.yml configuration has no `cooldown` block under either package-ecosystem entry (gomod at lines 4-10, github-actions at lines 14-20). Without a cooldown period, Dependabot will propose updates to newly published package versions immediately, including versions published only seconds/minutes ago, which increases exposure to malicious or unstable packages (e.g., supply-chain attacks where a compromised maintainer account publishes a malicious version that gets auto-proposed before it can be detected/pulled). This matches the rule's flagged condition exactly; the configuration file confirms the absence of any `cooldown:` key anywhere in the file.
